snippets.title: Trunk-based / GitHub Flow daily loop
snippets.code:
# morning
git switch main && git pull --ff-only
git switch -c feature/short-name

# ... work, commit small ...
git push -u origin feature/short-name

# open PR, get review, CI green
# merge button on the PR UI: 'Squash and merge' → branch auto-deleted

# back to main for the next thing
git switch main && git pull --ff-only
snippets.explanation: Same loop, multiple times a day. Branches rarely live more than a day or two.

snippets.title: Tag a release
snippets.code:
# Lightweight tag (just a name):
git tag v1.2.0

# Annotated tag (recommended — includes message, author, signature):
git tag -a v1.2.0 -m "Release 1.2.0: HNSW reranker, improved chunk overlap"

# Push the tag (tags don't push with normal git push):
git push origin v1.2.0

# List existing tags:
git tag --sort=-v:refname | head -10
snippets.explanation: Annotated tags carry metadata; lightweight tags are just bookmarks. Push tags explicitly.

snippets.title: GitFlow: hotfix off main, back-merge to develop
snippets.code:
# Customer hits a 500 in prod (currently v1.2.0):
git switch main && git pull --ff-only
git switch -c hotfix/1.2.1-login-500

# ... fix, test ...
git push -u origin hotfix/1.2.1-login-500
# open PR -> merge to main, tag v1.2.1, deploy

# Back-merge so the fix isn't lost in the next release:
git switch develop && git pull --ff-only
git merge --no-ff main
git push origin develop
snippets.explanation: Hotfixes start from `main` (matches prod) and must be merged back to `develop` (so the next release inherits the fix).

snippets.title: Recommended `.gitconfig` defaults for any workflow
snippets.code:
git config --global init.defaultBranch main
git config --global pull.ff only
git config --global merge.conflictStyle diff3
git config --global rerere.enabled true
git config --global push.default current
git config --global push.autoSetupRemote true
snippets.explanation: These six lines give you safer defaults regardless of which workflow your team picks.
